description: Ptac is a synthetic sigma70 promoter combining the trp -35 with the lacUV5 -10 and operator, giving a strong, LacI/IPTG-inducible promoter for E. coli expression.

references.authors: de Boer HA, Comstock LJ, Vasser M
references.title: The tac promoter: a functional hybrid derived from the trp and lac promoters
references.journal: Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A 80(1):21-25

references.authors: Brosius J, Erfle M, Storella J
references.title: Spacing of the -10 and -35 regions in the tac promoter. Effect on its in vivo activity

references.authors: Amann E, Ochs B, Abel KJ
references.title: Tightly regulated tac promoter vectors useful for the expression of unfused and fused proteins in Escherichia coli

label: Ptac
note: Ptac is a synthetic sigma70 promoter combining the trp -35 with the lacUV5 -10 and operator, giving a strong, LacI/IPTG-inducible promoter for E. coli expression.
sigma70 consensus -35 hexamer TTGACA at the 5' end; trp-derived in the tac hybrid.
sigma70 consensus Pribnow box TATAAT (-12..-7 register), derived from lacUV5 (the UV5/L8 mutation converts the wild-type lac -10 to consensus).
Transcription start site: the first A of the lac operator, 7 nt downstream of the -10 hexamer.

label: lac operator
note: Native lac operator O1 retained from lacUV5 downstream of the -10; binds LacI for IPTG-inducible repression.

functional_claims.label: Strong, LacI-repressible / IPTG-inducible synthetic trp/lacUV5 hybrid.

source.quote: Ptac is a synthetic sigma70 promoter combining the trp -35 with the lacUV5 -10 and operator, giving a strong, LacI/IPTG-inducible promoter for E. coli expression.
